# Orchestrator decomposition (M3.5): extract ProcessSupervisorService, TaskStateService, CadenceEngine

## Context

`ai/daemons/Orchestrator.mjs` (682 LOC, shipped via PR #11016 sub of #11009) currently mixes coordination with concrete business logic across multiple responsibilities. Audit triggered by operator architectural challenge 2026-05-09: *"i still challenge the orchestrator architecture and if it does too much."*

**Empirical responsibility map (verified line-by-line read):**

| Lines | Current responsibility | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1-138 (helpers) + 138-682 (class) | Total: 682 LOC | Class doing too much |
| ~150 of those | Core: lifecycle (`start`/`stop`/`poll`), `configure`, sweep dispatcher (`runMaintenanceCycle`/`runTaskCycle`), static config + collaborator declarations | **Stay** |
| ~200 lines | `runTask` (spawn + child.on lifecycle + PID-file write + state-mutation hooks) + `recoverTask`/`recoverTasks`/`watchRecoveredTask`/`clearRecoveredTask` + `getTaskPidFile` + `processCommand` | **Extract ŌåÆ ProcessSupervisorService** |
| ~60 lines | `readState`/`writeState`/`createInitialTaskState` (on-disk task-state envelope + JSON persistence) | **Extract ŌåÆ TaskStateService** |
| ~30 lines + per-task wiring | `shouldRunIntervalTask`/`parseInterval` + the boilerplate `runSummaryCycle`/`runKbSyncCycle` pattern (would repeat for every new task) | **Extract ŌåÆ CadenceEngine** |
| ~15 lines | `writeLog` (file-append + console mirror) | Use existing Neo logger or shared utility |

**Existing extractions that prove this is the right substrate pattern:**

- `ai/services/memory-core/HealthService.mjs` ŌĆö outcome recording
- `ai/daemons/services/SummarizationCoordinatorService.mjs` ŌĆö `getDueTask({...})` (sunset-handover + interval-sweep trigger logic for summary lane); already consumed via Orchestrator's `summarizationCoordinator_` reactive config at line 233
- `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon-queries.mjs` ŌĆö DB init + handover queries

**Architectural target (mirroring v13-path.md M2 "Common Base Server Class" pattern at the daemon tier):**

Orchestrator shrinks to ~150 LOC of pure coordination:
- Hold collaborators: `processSupervisor_`, `taskState_`, `cadenceEngine_`, `healthService_`, per-task coordinators
- Run poll loop, call `cadenceEngine.runIfDue(taskName, coordinator.getDueTask, processSupervisor.runTask)` per lane
- That's it

This is a NEW milestone in the v13 path: **M3.5 (Orchestrator decomposition)**, sequenced between current M3 (orchestrator skeleton, complete via #11009/#11016) and M4 (migrate decomposed daemon services to Orchestrator). Adding M3.5 first prevents M4's per-coordinator-service additions (DreamCoordinator, SandmanCoordinator, BackupService, GoldenPathCoordinator, GraphMaintenanceCoordinator) from compounding the existing fat-class problem.

## Sub-tasks (file lazily as work picks them up)

| # | Sub-extraction | Foundational order |
|---|---|---|
| Sub-1 | `ProcessSupervisorService` ŌĆö `ai/daemons/services/ProcessSupervisorService.mjs` (or `ai/services/memory-core/`); owns runTask + recoverTask family + PID-file lifecycle | First (largest extraction; everything else depends on it) |
| Sub-2 | `TaskStateService` ŌĆö `ai/daemons/services/TaskStateService.mjs`; owns readState/writeState/createInitialTaskState envelope | Parallel with Sub-1 |
| Sub-3 | `CadenceEngine` ŌĆö `ai/daemons/services/CadenceEngine.mjs`; owns shouldRunIntervalTask + parseInterval + the per-task `runIfDue(taskName, dueCheckFn, executeFn)` pattern | After Sub-1 + Sub-2 |
| Sub-4 | Orchestrator slim-down PR ŌĆö wire all three collaborators, drop extracted methods, verify no behavior regression | After Sub-1 + Sub-2 + Sub-3 |

## Acceptance Criteria (epic-level ŌĆö fires when sub-tickets close)

- [ ] AC1 ŌĆö `ProcessSupervisorService` Neo class extracted; subprocess spawn + lifecycle + PID-file recovery owned by service; Orchestrator consumes via `processSupervisor_` reactive collaborator
- [ ] AC2 ŌĆö `TaskStateService` Neo class extracted; readState/writeState owned by service; Orchestrator consumes via `taskState_` reactive collaborator
- [ ] AC3 ŌĆö `CadenceEngine` Neo class extracted; cadence math + per-task `runIfDue` boilerplate owned by service; Orchestrator consumes via `cadenceEngine_` reactive collaborator
- [ ] AC4 ŌĆö `Orchestrator.mjs` shrinks to ~150 LOC; only coordination logic remains
- [ ] AC5 ŌĆö All existing Orchestrator unit tests still pass (no behavior regression); new tests cover service-level contracts
- [ ] AC6 ŌĆö `learn/agentos/v13-path.md` updated to add M3.5 milestone between M3 and M4 (will be picked up by #11019)

## Avoided Traps

- ŌØī **Pile more tasks onto current Orchestrator shape** ŌĆö every new "add task X to Orchestrator" makes the fat-class problem worse; decompose first
- ŌØī **Big-bang single-PR rewrite** ŌĆö sub-tickets per extraction allow incremental review + cross-family verification per-extraction
- ŌØī **Skip the precedent audit** ŌĆö `SummarizationCoordinatorService` is the exemplar pattern; new extractions mirror it (Neo class with focused method surface, reactive collaborator-injection in Orchestrator)
- ŌØī **Sub-task locations without precedent verification** ŌĆö `ai/daemons/services/` already hosts SummarizationCoordinatorService + GraphMaintenanceService + GoldenPathSynthesizer + ConceptIngestor + ConceptDiscoveryService + GapInferenceEngine + IssueIngestor + LazyEdgeDrainer + MemorySessionIngestor + SemanticGraphExtractor + TopologyInferenceEngine ŌĆö that's the canonical home for daemon-tier services

## Provenance

- Operator architectural challenge 2026-05-09: "an orchestrator orchestrates ... i still challenge the orchestrator architecture and if it does too much"
- Triggered by my flawed framing of #11018 (closed as not planned 2026-05-09)
- Empirical anchor: `wc -l ai/daemons/Orchestrator.mjs` = 682; responsibility map verified via line-by-line read
- Substrate precedent: SummarizationCoordinatorService.mjs (already proves the pattern works in this codebase)
- v13-path.md M2 "Common Base Server Class" architectural pattern applied at daemon tier
